what_it_does: A Wave-3 adversarial design audit of the drafted loop-prep triad (COMPLETION_CRITERIA.md, IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md, RESEARCH_SYNTHESIS.md) plus OPEN-DECISIONS.md, checked against the governing laws, the 38-constraint ledger, the decided design, and the code-grounded merge frame. Its job is to find defects, gaps, risks, and decisions not yet given to Tim, not to confirm the docs are good.
observations: The file is structured as a skeptic's pass with sections A (most serious findings), B (law / constraint violations and silent gaps), C (pillar-promise vs delivered-design gaps), D (completion-criteria quality), E (new open decisions), F (smaller tightenings), and G (what is solid).
It uses a three-tier severity system: red (serious pillar/law violation), orange (real gap), yellow (hygiene / tightening).
Every finding is tagged as [Observed] (read directly) or [Inferred] (reasoned consequence) and phrased as an action.

It identifies six red findings: A1 merge/omniscience build list not carried into criteria, A2 switch-flip test can pass on near-empty corpus, A3 pooled-concept criterion under-tests completeness/recall, A4 grounded-chain law not made verifiable, A5 central judge D-2 unresolved yet criteria depend on it, A6 tool_result lazy-fetch assumes fetchability without verification.

Section B catalogs law violations and silent gaps for Classification Law, edge directionality, Convergence Law, no-silent-failures, no-fiction-about-Tim, extraction-vs-judgment, not-agent-architecture-by-default, and context-package design.

Section C maps Pillar 1 and Pillar 2 promises to weak or missing criteria, including floor injection quality, project-structure scale, merge inventory vs merge operation, unified capture path, Company-interface dependency, non-CC modalities, and learning from corrections.
Section D applies verify-by-use and two-faced (Function + Form) scrutiny, listing criteria passable by code-existence, missing Form faces, and the absent B5 fresh-agent criterion.

Section E introduces eight new open decisions (D-NEW-1 through D-NEW-8) with stated leans, covering floor injection strategy, legacy lane retirement, retrieval-completeness contract, derivation-4 growth law, search/read naming, merge scope, non-CC modality sequencing, and unattended-judge dependency.
Section F contains smaller tightenings such as G0.1 isolation rationale, G1.2 backfill counts, G3.4 re-embed verification, G2.1 negative test, and a phase-boundary inconsistency.

Section G briefly notes what the audit considers solid, including the extraction-vs-judgment spine, registry-is-truth, re-embed refusal, lazy tool_result decision, and questionless-sweep identification.
A routing note states the audit routes findings back to the planner and does not block, but red items and new open decisions should reach Tim before the build loop starts.
standouts: A1-A6: six serious findings where pillar promises or laws are unbuildable or unverified at scale.
D-NEW-1 through D-NEW-8: a set of implied but unlisted open decisions that change what gets built or what 'green' means.
The repeated pattern of laws asserted in prose/FORM-adjectives without a runnable floor or negative test (B-law-1 through B-law-8).
The insistence that Pillar-2 omniscience requires a populated edge graph and a sweep-based completeness verdict, not a single hand-made edge or top-k pool.
The absence of a fresh-agent-drives-real-task criterion despite it being a top-priority constraint (B5 / A4).
conventions: Uses severity emojis (red/orange/yellow) and evidence tags ([Observed] / [Inferred]) consistently.
References specific files, sections, and criterion IDs (e.g., G4.2, G10.3, D-2) to ground findings.
Phrases every finding as an action rather than a complaint.
Follows a routing convention: audit routes to planner, does not block.
Maintains a skeptical, defect-seeking tone while ending with a calibrated 'what is solid' section.
No ranking of files or winners; it is a local audit only.
concerns: The audit is dense and could overwhelm a planner if not triaged; many findings are inferred consequences rather than observed doc text.
Some findings assume knowledge of external files (laws, ledger, merge frame) that are not reproduced here.
The line between 'missing criterion' and 'design gap' is sometimes blurred, which may require Tim to separate build work from documentation work.
notes: A skeptical, law-driven audit that converts high-level design promises into a checklist of runnable, population-scale, negative-tested criteria and eight new open decisions before build starts.
questions: What is the exact process by which these findings are triaged and turned into updated criteria or open decisions?
Which of the inferred consequences have already been discussed with Tim and which are genuinely new?
How will the proposed negative tests (e.g., kill a lens mid-run, fresh-agent run) be executed and recorded?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to find what is wrong, missing, or law-violating, not to confirm the docs are good; the file does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Law-driven design with deterministic floors and negative tests.
No silent failures and grounded-chain ergonomics.
Honest scope: inventory-for-agent vs automated merge, precomputed floor vs live SessionStart.
Population-scale proofs rather than single-instance existence checks.
intention_signals: The file is trying to force every pillar promise and governing law into a concrete, runnable, real-corpus criterion with a negative test, and to surface all implied decisions before the build loop starts so that 'green' status cannot be gamed.
novelty: It introduces an explicit audit layer that distinguishes observed from inferred evidence, assigns severity, and generates new open decisions with stated leans, rather than merely reviewing prose.
contribution: A catalog of concrete defects, law-verification holes, pillar-promise gaps, and implied open decisions with severity and suggested actions, which a fused system would keep as a pre-build verification checklist and decision sheet.
summary_for_embedding: Wave-3 adversarial design audit of the recollection loop-prep triad and open decisions, enumerating serious criteria gaps, law-verification holes, pillar-promise defects, and new open decisions.
intention_for_embedding: To surface unbuildable or under-tested design claims and force them into runnable, population-scale, negative-tested criteria before the build loop begins.
